     The Numbers Business Report

Sound of Hope: The Story of Possum Trot (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $11,647,099Details
International Box Office $121,490
Worldwide Box Office $11,768,589
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Donna and Reverend Martin ignite a fire in the hearts of their rural church to embrace kids in the foster system that nobody else would take. By doing the impossible–adopting 77 children–this East Texas community proved that, with real, determined love, the battle for America’s most vulnerable can be won.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$3,078,758 (26.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.78 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:99.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$8,500,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,200 opening theaters/2,200 max. theaters, 2.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $12,316,468

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 4th, 2024 (Wide) by Angel Studios
International Releases: October 11th, 2024 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 31st, 2024 (Limited), released as Som da Esperança: A História de Possum Trot (Brazil)
March 13th, 2025 (Limited), released as O Som da Esperança (Portugal)
June 20th, 2025 (Limited), released as Sonido de Esperanza: El Milagro de Possum Trot (Spain)
January 8th, 2026 (Limited) (Germany)
Video Release: September 10th, 2024 by Angel Studios
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material involving child abuse, some violence, language and brief suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2832 (cert #54976), 5/1/2024)
Running Time: 129 minutes
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Georgia, United States
Geographical setting: Texas
Plot point: Child Abuse
Social setting: Foster Family
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Angel Studios, Peacetree Productions, The Daily Wire
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Donna and Reverend Martin ignite a fire in the hearts of their rural church to embrace kids in the foster system that nobody else would take. By doing the impossible–adopting 77 children–this East Texas community proved that, with real, determined love, the battle for America’s most vulnerable can be won.
